strings.SS2_EQUIP_BACKTHRUSTER_LORE:
<style=cMono>Audio transcription complete from signal echoes. Assigning generic tokens.</style>

MAN 1: This is a terrible idea, you know.
[rattling metal]

MAN 2: You say that about every idea I have.

MAN 1: Because all of your ideas are terrible.

[engine sputtering]

MAN 2: Rrgh, oh, c'mon, then! You were working fine just a minute ago.

MAN 1: Maybe it knows it's attached to your back, and not to a piece of cargo.

MAN 2: Oh, good point! I bet these have a safety on them of some sort... Ah, ther it is.

[click]

MAN 1: I... Shouldn't have said anything.

MAN 2: Nah, this is great! Alright, count me down.

MAN 1 : Ugh... Alright. Three-

[thruster blasting off]

<style=cMono>End of requested transcript.</style>

Order: Cloaking Headband
Tracking Number: 554*****
Estimated Delivery: 08/01/2056
Shipping Method: Standard
Shipping Address: 202 W. Calbury Ln, Adora, Mercury
Shipping Details:

I was finally able to get ahold of a personal cloaking device for you. Sneaking around in places you shouldn't be should be a cinch with this puppy.

Now, there's a couple a' things you should understand about this thing. First, the cloaking only lasts a short time since the device is so small, so keep yourself hidden while it cools down again. Secondly, you need to hit the button on the headband to activate it. Not exactly a very clandestine action, I know, but it's what I could find for you.

Last, if the box you get seems like it's completely empty, just feel around in the bottom until you find the button. It saves time, money, and privacy to ship it without the Volatile treatment. Tell me when this gets to you, and have fun with your new toy.

strings.SS2_EQUIP_GREATERWARBANNER_LORE:
<style=cMono>Recording from the Terran Museum of Tactics and Warfare.</style>

Some say that one of the most important resources on the battlefield is morale. The functionality of a unit is exponentially increased depending on the mood of the troops within it. This can be raised or lowered through various means. For example, the quality of their lodging or food, news they've heard from their home, or even spending time recreationally.

However, one tried and true method of raising the morale of a unit is the usage of symbolism. Many armies would wear colors and designs symbolizing their beliefs and their strength. Many units would carry flags in order to keep their morale high in combat.

The usage of this method has also seen variations. While a flag bearer for a unit will increase morale, it has been observed that flags unique to higher ranking military will further increase the unit's morale. Should a Colonel or General's flag appear on the battlefield, the troops will be encouraged by the power they display, and in turn increase their own fighting prowess.

strings.SS2_ITEM_SHACKLEDLAMP_LORE:
"Excuse me."

"Yes, over here, please. I need to ask you something."

"Closer, now."

"I need your help. This lantern you see before you, it is merely a fractured piece of a greater body."

"The journey? Ah, yes. It will be long, but do not fret. I will guide you wherever you must go, and, while it may be fraught with danger, it will be nothing I cannot assist you with."

"Hm? What's in it for you, you say?"

"Oh, you will see. Your reward will be far greater than you could possibly imagine. Once I have returned to my full splendor, I will protect you. I will keep you safe. Keep you warm. Make you powerful. All your worries will be burned away. Doesn't that sound wonderful?"

"You agree? Good. Now, pick up the lantern, quickly."

"We have a long journey ahead of us, friend."
strings.SS2_ITEM_STIRRINGSOUL_DESC: Slain monsters leave a <style=cIsUtility>soul</style> which has a <style=cIsUtility>{0}%</style> <style=cStack>(+{0}% per unsuccessful roll, resetting on success)</style> chance to <style=cIsUtility>spawn an item</style> on pickup.
strings.SS2_ITEM_STIRRINGSOUL_LORE:
A spirit of determination...
<link="textWavy">...A spirit to be stopped.</link>
An unwavering will to survive...
<link="textWavy">...A flickering will to survive.</link>

Willing to desecrate their identity...
<link="textWavy">...Willing to desecrate their memory.</link>
Willing to sacrifice their civilization...
<link="textWavy">...Willing to sacrifice their humanity.</link>

To what lengths will they go to live?
<link="textWavy">To what lengths will they go to bring themselves ruin?</link>
To what lengths will they go to escape?
<link="textWavy">To what lengths will they go to destroy?</link>

All in the name of self preservation...
<link="textWavy">...All in the name of narcissism.</link>
All for the sake of an uncertain hope...
<link="textWavy">...All for the sake of a null chance.</link>

What would happen if given an opportunity?
<link="textWavy">What would happen if given an ally?</link>
What would happen if their fire was stoked?
<link="textWavy">What would happen if they were given false hope?</link>

Maybe they should be given a chance.
<link="textWavy">Maybe they should be given strength.</link>
Maybe they should be given an offer.
<link="textWavy">Maybe they should be taken first.</link>

strings.SS2_ITEM_REMUNERATION_LORE:
<link="textShaky">What is your legacy?</link>

Nothing but petty vengeance.

<link="textShaky">What is your identity?</link>

Nothing but a name spoken in disgust.

<link="textShaky">What do you want?</link>

Nothing but a new purpose.

<link="textShaky">What do you need?</link>

Nothing but the weapons that I trust.

<link="textShaky">So, where will you go?</link>

Wherever you make me go.

<link="textShaky">Then, what will you do?</link>

Whatever you say you need.

<link="textShaky">Is that all, then?</link>

If you say it so.

<link="textShaky">Then go there, and die.</link>

...

As long as you're paying me.

strings.SS2_ITEM_RELICOFDUALITY_LORE:
Hmm? Oh, Youngling! You are back sooner than expected. Have you completed your search?

Oh, dear. Youngling, why are you shivering? Has something hurt you?

You found something that hurt you? Hmm. Peculiar. Do you have it with you, still?

...I see. Let me look at it, then. Come now, and show me what you've found.

Hmm. A strange thing, this one. A blazing fire, yet cold to the touch. How unnatural, this is. There's a certain harmony to it, however. As the heat brings warmth and life, the cold tempers it, and keeps it in check. But in that same way, that bitter cold may bring despair and death without the heat to stave it off.

...Oh, but there I go, rambling once more. You need to be taken care of, Youngling. Please, come with me.
strings.SS2_ITEM_RELICOFEXTINCTION_PICKUP: A black hole follows you that deals extreme damage, BUT it can damage you.
strings.SS2_ITEM_RELICOFFORCE_DESC: All attacks strike <style=cIsDamage>1</style> <style=cStack>(+1 per stack)</style> additional time for <style=cIsDamage>{1}%</style> TOTAL damage. <style=cIsUtility>Consecutive strikes</style> to the same enemy increase damage dealt by <style=cIsDamage>{1}%</style>, up to a maximum of <style=cIsDamage>{2}%</style> TOTAL damage dealt. <style=cDeath>Reduce attack speed and increase skill cooldowns by {0}%</style> <style=cStack>(-{0}% per stack)</style>.
strings.SS2_ITEM_RELICOFFORCE_PICKUP: All attacks strike an additional time. <style=cDeath>Lowered attack speed and increased skill cooldowns</style>.
strings.SS2_ITEM_RELICOFFORCE_LORE:
Ah, Youngling! You have returned once more, with what seems to be a full bag, too! Full of scavengings, yes?

Hmm. The pack is heavy, but it is nearly empty. You... You are not hiding things from me, Youngling?

...You found something very heavy. I see. Forgive me for my suspicions. Show me this thing you have found.

Ah! You are correct, Youngling. This thing is small, but difficult to hold. It splinters and fragments, and each piece is just as heavy as the whole. What a remarkable thing, this is!

I will put it with the other wonderful things you have found. A thing like this could be dangerous if it fell into the wrong hands. As for you, Youngling... Hmm, I believe some repairs for your bag are in order. Come with me.
strings.SS2_ITEM_RELICOFMASS_DESC: <style=cIsHealing>Increase maximum health</style> by <style=cIsHealing>{0}%</style> <style=cStack>(+{0}% per stack)</style>. <style=cDeath>Reduce acceleration and deceleration</style> by a factor of <style=cDeath>{1}</style> <style=cStack>(+{1} per stack)</style>.
strings.SS2_ITEM_RELICOFMASS_PICKUP: Double your max health. <style=cDeath>Your movement has momentum</style>.
strings.SS2_ITEM_RELICOFMASS_LORE:
Ah, welcome back! Youngling, what have you found?

Hmmm. More scavengings from the blasted heath? Very well. You may keep those things, if you wish. Have you found anything else?

Ah, you have? Let me see it then, youngling.

Oh! This shape that you've found! It is stalwart, but not loyal. Powerful, but yet also frail at the same time. It is a peculiar shape, to say the least of it. Where did you find this, youngling?

...You found it on the planet? Hmm. Worrisome. This was not created by those sandfolk. Too complex, too powerful. Nor by those Lemurians. Tell me, youngling, you did not steal this, did you?

...You did not. I see. Very well. I would like to keep this shape. This shape is dangerous if you do not understand it.

Oh, my youngling, do not look so saddened. This shape is remarkable, and I laud your work regardless of this. I simply want to keep it safe. Here, perhaps a reward is in order. Come with me.
strings.SS2_ITEM_RELICOFTERMINATION_DESC: Every <style=cIsUtility>{0} seconds</style>, grants a buff which <style=cIsDamage>marks</style> the next spawning enemy. If defeated within <style=cIsUtility>30 seconds</style> <style=cStack>(-10% per stack)</style>, they <style=cIsDamage>drop an item</style> <style=cStack>(item rarity scales with stacks)</style>; if not, they drop <style=cIsDamage>nothing</style> and delay the next mark. <style=cDeath>Marked enemies have significantly increased stats</style>.
strings.SS2_ITEM_RELICOFTERMINATION_PICKUP: Every {0} seconds, mark an enemy and strengthen them. If defeated in time, they drop an item; if not, they drop nothing.
strings.SS2_ITEM_RELICOFTERMINATION_LORE:
Youngling! Ah, I am so excited to see you return! Have you found anything new?

Oh? You have! Do show me, little one. There's no need to be shy.

Hm... What have you found this time? It's sturdy and hollow, but when you shake it... it sloshes, like an overripe nutapple. What a strange shape we have here...

Oh? Oh my. You were chased by a monster, you say... It did not follow you here, correct?

...It did not. But of course, it would be hard to find us here, wouldn't it, Youngling?

Here, I will take this away. Somewhere where it can bring no monsters to us.

...I understand. It is okay to be afraid sometimes, but you cannot let it overwhelm you. Would it not be cruel to another wanderer to find this shape, and suffer dearly for it?

We will be safe here, Youngling. Come with me. You must be hungry after being out for so long.

strings.SS2_ITEM_PRIMAL_BIRTHRIGHT_LORE:
The symbols on the parchment are foreign to you. You do not understand what they are, but somehow, you understand the meaning they carry. Power. Will. Authority, and a message. 

"The right to rule is earned through strength and wit, and those alone. It cannot be granted by another. It cannot be granted by birth nor blood. Only strength justifies itself."
You look back down. The flickering lights of the hull cast strange shapes in the shard. Reflected shadows against its glow, coalescing into shapes that coalesce into figures.

Two shadows stumble aimlessly around in the reflection, against the backdrop of dawn. Short, stubby shadows. A seed falls into one's hair, and it laughs and plants it back into the soil. A seed falls into another's hands. It gently pries the thing open, to learn what will grow.

The shadows lengthen as the sun moves through the sky. Long, imposing shadows against twilight. Positioned perfectly, like an eerie gate to the light.

One shadow begins to grow faster still, stretching until the whole world is a blanket of darkness. The lone shadow thrashes against its prison, indistinguishable against the sea of blackness, fiercely refusing to fade away.

One shadow remains in the light. It stands in front of the now rising sun, eclipsing its light. Rays of golden sun wrap around it, forming a coronal halo for the shadow. Standing out starkly against the world around it- blinding, searing, formless. Selfish. It does not move.

You squint your eyes, and the symbols hold a new meaning.

"It isn't fair."

</style>This is the logbook of D. Furthen, naturalist aboard the UES [Redacted] in conjunction with the ongoing UES Research and Documentation of Outer Life program. This entry was written at Point Sigma, during an expedition to the dense jungles of Cornea III.

---------------------

I have encountered my first sample of outer life on this planet! What a marvel it is, too. The sample resembles an earthen roundworm, but significantly bigger. I have assigned their common name as 'Detritive Trematode', and will describe their properties below.

• Roughly two inches in diameter, and varying in size from about four inches to a foot and a half in length. White coloration.

• These trematodes were found feasting on a pile of rotting meat in a damp cavern. The meat was of indeterminate origin, but the presence of other, larger animals is exciting nonetheless.

• Upon scooping up a clump of the rotting meat, the trematodes quickly reacted to the movement and began wriggling around, before slowly relaxing. The guard positioned with me, Chevry, I think, was disgusted.

• Upon touching one with an ungloved hand to feel their texture, one of the trematodes latched onto my palm and began chewing. It was extremely painful, both to have the trematode on my hand, and when Chevry cut the thing off. Despite that, other than a light gash on my palm, I believe I am fine.

</style>"So this book's the only thing in the storage container?"
"Pretty much. Other than some old furniture, this is it."
"So what's this book's deal? If this is all we're getting, it better be good."
"It's a diary, I think. First written in by one Alan Howizer, about 26 years ago. Seemed like a pretty smart guy, said he worked at a chemical testing plant back on Earth."
"That's it? This is just some old guy's diary?"
"Hold on, there's more to it. While I was flipping through, about a hundred pages in, the handwriting changed, and sure enough, it was found by a different person on a park bench, apparently. Same thing again, about thirty pages later. And again, after about another seventy. And it keeps on going. There's a couple dozen different people who wrote in this diary, writing down their fond memories, odd mysteries, riddles, their answers to those riddles, all sorts of things."
"Huh. This thing must be a real treasure trove of knowledge then, yeah? Any clue as to who last owned it?"
"Uh, yeah, actually. Someone named Chel was the last one to write in the book, and it looks like they left an address inside the back cover. Why, what are you thinking?"

<style=cMono>End of notable section.</style>

<style=cMono>Documentation from Research Station Rook

Specimen-18305 Week 3 Notes</style>

This is one of the more odd samples we've found. This fungus has seemed to grow on nearly everything in the building if we let it. The walls, floors, ceiling. They seem to have a preference for things that have been moving. Our clothes, doors, and even some machinery. We will continue to monitor this behavior.

<style=cMono>Specimen-18305 Week 5 Notes</style>

The subject has continued to attach itself to things around the lab. We've noted that it becomes healthier the more it moves with its attached object. Tests seem to indicate it subsists off of the heat created by movements, taken in by its roots. We will attempt to transplant it to a consistently moving machine and monitor its health.

<style=cMono>Specimen-18305 Week 8 Notes</style>

We've left the sample attached to an old model of piston from the lab. In case of any damage, we had intended to dispose of it regardless. We had attached it and left it running the previous 3 weeks. Most curiously, the piston was assumed to break down in this time frame. However, it would seem the subject has created a symbiotic relationship with the machine. We will continue to monitor this behavior for more negative effects, and potential other uses for this specimen.

strings.SS2_ITEM_MALICE_LORE:
You are weak.

Physically? Perhaps not. You fight to survive, and you fight well enough. But in the mind? My friend, you are frail. You fight and kill, but feel remorse for that which you kill. Why? The animals, they do not feel remorse towards you. To them, you are but prey to stalk, or predator to flee from. Those hulking titans? They bear no emotion, only a silent directive to destroy. To destroy you.

And I know you. My friend, you do not wish to be weak. You wish to be powerful, in mind as well as in body. Do not feel pity for these mindless beasts, for they are just that. Fight to kill, not just to survive.

But man's malice can only take you so far. Take this. Be pulled further. Fight to kill, and kill to be feared.

Animals live, and animals die. They take from the land, and give back in equal portion. The vicious cycle of life continues its oroborrean trajectory-

-and all of the while, the fungus chuckles.

Then, man came. He built empires. Persisted through peace and prosperity, and through famine and war. Even still, the center never holds. Empires rise, and empires fall-

-and all the while, the fungus chuckles.

Gods could be born. Paradise could be founded. It does not matter. Brotherhoods shatter like glass, and death reigns even in Arcadia. Entire worlds fall fallow-

-and all the while, the fungus chuckles.

Indeed, nothing lasts forever. Star systems, galaxies, the whole universe, even. All will submit to entropy, given time. All is ephemeral, all will be dust-

-and all the while, the fungus chuckles.

<style=cMono>//--Auto-Transcription from room K3 (Kitchen, 3rd Floor) of Kaymar Hotel--//</style>

A tall, thin man walks into the room, holding a large cardboard box. "Hey, CHEF. How's it going?" He sets the box on the counter. "Alright, we've got a bunch of new sauces you'll have to get accustomed with. I'll walk you through them now, if you don't mind." The CHEF bot gives him its attention, but continues to work.

The man proceeds down the rows of various colored bottles, explaining to the CHEF their names, flavors, and uses in cooking. Eventually, he stops at a red-colored bottle, and hesitates briefly.

"...Okay, now. This bottle? It's unlabelled for a reason. If you get a meal order stating that you use "<style=cDeath>Hottest Sauce</style>" on it, you go ahead and use this bottle. But do NOT use it under ANY OTHER CIRCUMSTANCES. I know sometimes you need to improvise to finish a dish, but you should NEVER use this bottle if you're doing that. Do you understand?"

The CHEF bot, still working, nods affirmatively.

"Good. I expect great things from you, CHEF."

The CHEF bot nods once again, and briefly pauses its work to put the bottles in the appropriate locations.

"...Oh, and also?"

The CHEF bot pauses to give its attention to the man once more.

"Please, don't break that bottle. I know you're a robot and all, but I also know that accidents happen, and that particular accident might leave a hole in the floor."

The CHEF bot gazes at the bottle briefly, before carefully setting it in the back of the cupboard with the rest of the sauces.

strings.SS2_ITEM_JETBOOTS_LORE:
Record for the postmortem of Hephaestus Metal Works

Location: Hephaestus Metal Works, Mars
Location Status: Interior destroyed via fire
Casualties: The owner, via fire
On Site Report:

I'd assumed the cause of the fire was the work of a forge, furnace, or even a cigarette break. There wasn't much different from any other workshop of the same niche. A bunch of old kinds of swords, armor, farming equipment, et cetera. Though it would seem the forget was a hobbyist himself, as there were what looked to be some projects you normally wouldn't find there in the back.

A fair amount of them were nothing more than motors and metal. He seemed to practice making clockwork. But he eventually moved on to fuel and engine based items. Old lawn mowers, motor cycles, and generators. He eventually moved on to propulsion systems. Small remote planes, helicopters, an old rusty jetpack. It seemed he was working up to something, and we found what it was in the back.

There was clearly some genius behind him. He was building a pair of jet boots from scratch. They haven't entered production before, since jetpacks do the job fine, and boots can't be as sturdy and heavy while getting the same amount of thrust. He seemed to have the same thought, as he made them more sturdy for his protection, but tried to compensate by turning up the output on the thrust. The result ended in the heat building up and erupting from below him. Unfortunately, his inventive nature cost him his business and his life.

The surrounding area didn't take any damage so the shop may be renovated soon. The boots have been confiscated and are en route to be studied and decommissioned, and the remaining items from inside the shop have been sent to their next of kin.

This concludes my report.

Two figures sit, one shivering among a sea of rubble in a frozen burrow. The only lights given are the small remains of a fire, and old sparking wires. Barely any protection against the biting cold.

"Hey..." He looks up, distantly hopeful. "Do you think we'll ever make it off this rock?"

"Hard to say." She takes a bite of a ration she found.

"Well there has to be something we've overlooked, right? Some method we just haven't thought of yet?" He stands up, and begins pacing around anxiously.

"Could be." Another bite.

"Maybe there's some wreckage with working parts we just haven't found yet, or a problem in the current ones we could fix?" A shine returning to his eyes, getting himself more hopeful and excited.

"Definitely possible." Another bite.

He stops as a disgusted look crosses his face. "Ugh, could you please help me think of something instead of just eating?"

"I wouldn't worry about it." Another bite.

His disgust quickly gives way to blatant frustration and anger. "Why not?! Don't you want to get out of here?!"

She sighs, and runs a hand through her hair. "Well sure. But that doesn't mean we've gotta stress over every little thing. We're alive, ain't we? We're lucky we have that much."

"Well... I suppose you're right... It'd be bad to get worked up right now..." His expression relaxes as he looks back to the ground.

"See? Just sit down, relax, and let my old stash warm you up." Another bite, as she passes the can.

"My friends. I have gathered you all here today to share some important news.

First, it pains my heart to announce that Nkota, the last of our great leaders, our kind matriarch, has sadly passed on from this life. As far as we know, she took her last breath peacefully in rest last night, full of years and with all business here finished. With that said, there shall be no one to succeed her in leadership. She was the last of her line, with no living brothers, sisters, or children.

Instead, in her final testament, her wisdom has created a new beginning. For us. For all of us. Her wish is that the remaining members of this community spread out, either together or separately, into the world. It is perhaps a drastic option, but one that I, personally, also believe to be the best for this community.

Nkota shall be buried at our final destination, as is her will. You all may grieve for her and this community for a time, but do not tarry. A group of pilgrims will travel with myself to the great grey path, fifty miles west, after a week's time. You may follow, or go on your own way. It is your choice entirely. If you do not think that you can make the journey, do not fret. Nkota may have had no children, yes, but her heritage is in spirit, not in blood. You are all her children, and she will protect you until you reach your final goal.

Thank you all for your time. I would suggest you begin to pack your possessions for the journey ahead."

 - Eulogy for Nkota and the Village of Dago

Eighty seconds. Damned lizards. They're more cunning than they seemed.

Seventy seconds. She took a moment to breathe. What had once been harsh winds was no still air, contained within the miniature biosphere.

Sixty seconds. She counted her bullets.

Fifty seconds. Two remaining magazines, a box-worth of shells, one grenade - waterlogged, and a duled knife. Not nearly enough.

Forty seconds. She counted their heads.

Thirty seconds. The lizards gave her shield a wide berth. Either they were frightened of this new technology, or... they were waiting her out.

Twenty seconds. She loaded her shotgun.

Ten seconds. The lizards charged - and she hoped she was ready.

strings.SS2_ITEM_SKATEBOARD_LORE:
Wheels, rolling against unhewn stone. Unrefined, uncoordinated technique- irresponsible and careless in equal measure.

"You are doing it wrong."

"I am doing this as I please! This is an art, you know, Brother."

"You've failed countless times already."

"We'll see."

Focused eyes, darting as stars reflecting the sky. Lightning flashes against the remote landscape in the distance. Twinkling thunderbolts catching a stray eye. A single lapse of focus, then an impact. A frustrated scoff. Glares, mutual and infuriated, sharp enough to cut through the gap in the air preceding oncoming thunder.

"I'm beginning to have doubts, Brother."

"..well, I've yet to give up! How could I have failed?"

Impact, skidding quick enough to sear against the kicked-up dust. Shaky limbs, rising from the ground. Defiance. Potential energy, mounting into heat and purpose, tangible in the air. Suffocating tension, louder than thunder. It all comes crashing down.

"Another failure."

"..you know, I don't believe I've failed once, Brother. There's something to be said of art about the simple act of learning."

"You HAVE failed! Each and every time you have tried you have failed, and I have attempted to teach you- but you do not listen. I grow tired of teaching and tired of watching!"

Wheels begin to roll against stone again. They hum and kick like an organ attuned to the pulse of the Earth underfoot, barely holding on.

"You incessantly add your own additions to even my simplest instructions.."

Forward movement. The thrill of velocity in one's chest. Of feeling your blood and senses rush back, jostled by inertia. A reminder of truly being alive.

"When you're done playing with all this, allow me to demonstrate what I've tried to teach."

Uncontrollable energy, wild and frenzied- forces converging together at last. A perfect storm.
Rotation, freed of an axis and speed- that essential rush of heat and velocity.
Lightning. Raw energy, full of potential- now fully realized.
Joy and astonishment and elation. Shock. Satisfaction. Excitement. A third voice, calling out impatiently over the Venusian storm.

"Come in, boys- dinner's ready and it's no weather to be skateboarding in!"

AS FOR YOU, LITTLE ONES, I HAVE A GIFT FOR EACH AND EVERY ONE OF YOU. COME CLOSER.

Oh, dear. Look at you, poor thing.

You're freezing.

Here, now. You've been through so much already.

...

There we go. Your flame has reignited. That will keep you warm, for now.

Come with me. I'll introduce you to the others.

Oh? Of course there are others. It'd be cruel to be the only one of your kind, wouldn't it?

Come, now! Don't tarry. They'll share their warmth with you. Just follow me.

You'll be safe here.

strings.SS2_RUNSHROOM_BODY_LORE:
We awoke in a panic, our tent ablaze. Our equipment a molten mass of the periodic table.

"H-hey, over there! That thing!"

It was so small, it looked like it could be a bug under our shoe, yet the mess it made... There was no other way.

"Stay here, I'm gonna go squeeze that thing to a fuckin' pulp."

I sat there and watched as my crew lept like frogs at the small fungi, unable to even graze its surface. Profanities like a siren in the air.

Our efforts were fruitless. I signaled for the crew to return to aid in the dowsing of the flames.

"Pipe down and get over here! The fire ain't dyin' by itself! The bastard's like a roach, you won't catch it!"

The damages weren't covered.
strings.SS2_MIMIC_BODY_LORE:
"Now, I'd like to see this as something of a learning experience, all things considered. Evolutionary biology has a curious place for mimicry, my friend- certain creatures seek not to be threatening or inviting by their own merit, but rather by the means of seeming to be a different thing entirely. Of course, evolution being random means that this isn't what we're dealing with-"

"It’s also not what we're dealing with because-"

"No no, let me finish. In this case we have a very fascinating example, don't we? A machine emulating another machine in such an animated way, as a means to trick us into getting close- why, it's positively Wicklerian in design, isn't it? Whoever made this thing really had a heart for it, don't you think?"

"...Dude, it just steals shit. Nothin' fancy about it."

"Nature really is beautiful, even in a place like this."

"Shut up and let’s keep moving. Ugh, god, why'd I have to get stuck with this guy?"

-Signal echoes, UES Contact Light

strings.SS2_BORG_LORE:
<style=cMono>Audio file found under "Dr. Rayell's Testimony". Playing...</style>

"Recording started. Begin testing, Dr. Rayell."

"Thank you. After the EOD's smashing success with biomechanical augmentations, producing some of the finest combat engineers to ever come out of the department, they proceeded further with slightly more... shall we say, experimental designs."

"How so?"

"The department began looking into mental augmentation. It was definitely a leap, to be sure. Augmentation was already very cutting edge, but supplementing - or even replacing a damaged brain? It was a tall order. But the heads thought we could do it, so we obliged."

"Hm. What came of your project?"

"Once prototyping was complete, we began the human trials. We got all sorts of folks as test subjects, they were vegetative, mentally unstable, broken people. We were told to fix them as best would could."

"And how did that go?"

"It was... It was a travesty. Some of them were simply too far gone. There was nothing we could do. Those were the best cases. Worse was when they rejected the replacements. Some died in a swift immune response. The more cognisant ones begged for us to end the headaches they were having. Some of them just started screaming. They'd scream for hours and hours, sometimes for days even. And then they'd stop. They'd literally scream themselves to death."

"That's, um, a bit unsettling. Is that all?"

"There was one. One who made it through the replacement. And it worked wonderfully. We got him in a vegetative state, no hope whatsoever, and we took him from that, and we gave him - maybe not his full life back, but perhaps something resembling it. You have no idea how thankful I was to hear that he was doing well."

"Well, I suppose that's good to hear. So, what's happened to him?"

"You know? I'm not sure. Other than some behavioral changes, which is to be expected, he was able to return to society rather gracefully. We've done check-ups on him every three months to analyze long-term effects, but... Actually? Now that I'm thinking about it, he didn't show up for it last month. Oh, well. I'm sure he's doing fine. I'll have to contact him about that."

"Um, alright, then. Thank you for your time, Dr. Rayell. Your testimony is... Well, it's certainly something. I hope to hear more about your projects in the future."

"Very well. I hope you find what you're looking for."

<style=cMono>End of file.</style>

“Once, a world was formed. A mother and her two sons dwelt upon it.

The world was a reflection of its settlers. The mother would ensure her sons would learn the ways of this world, just as she shaped into something they could build upon. And so, the sons grew wise and strong, and the world flourished...“

“...As the sons grew old, so did their mother. Soon came time for her to depart - but before she did, she left two gifts. One for her sons, and one for the world.

For the sons she was leaving, she gave the gift of ambition and compassion. Together, they would achieve anything they could imagine. They would make paradise. For the world she was leaving, she created life. The world's first true natives. They would be the world's caretakers, and together, its very essence...“

- Galactic Fables, Volumes I and III

strings.SS2_CHIRR_BEFRIEND_DESCRIPTION: Spit neurotoxin that deals <style=cIsDamage>240% damage per second</style> and <style=cHumanObjective>befriends</style> under 50% health. Friends <style=cIsUtility>inherit items</style> and <style=cDeath>wither over time</style>.
strings.SS2_CHIRR_BEFRIEND_SCEPTER_DESCRIPTION:
Spit neurotoxin that deals <style=cIsDamage>240% damage per second</style> and <style=cHumanObjective>befriends</style> under 50% health. Friends <style=cIsUtility>inherit items</style> and <style=cDeath>wither over time</style>. 
<color=#d299ff>SCEPTER: Your friend follows you between stages, and no longer withers.</color>
strings.SS2_CHIRR_DETONATE_DESCRIPTION: <style=cIsUtility>Replaces Special.</style>. Spit a detonative agent at your <style=cHumanObjective>friend</style>, causing them to explode for <style=cIsDamage>1000-4000% damage</style> based on how long they were <style=cHumanObjective>befriended</style>.
strings.SS2_CHIRR_LEASH_DESCRIPTION: Tap to bring your ally to you. Hold to <style=cIsHealth>siphon health</style> from your friend.
strings.SS2_CHIRR_HEADBUTT_DESCRIPTION: <style=cIsUtility>Stunning</style>. Headbutt enemies in front of you <style=cIsDamage>{0}% damage</style>.
strings.SS2_CHIRR_HEAL_DESCRIPTION: Heal yourself and nearby allies for <style=cIsHealing>{0}% of their total health</style> and <style=cIsHealing>{1}% more over time</style>.
strings.SS2_KEYWORD_CYBORG_PRIME: <color=#42f5b3><style=cKeywordName>Primed</style></color><style=cSub>Hitting <color=#42f5b3>Primed</color> enemies with a different attack deals an additional <style=cIsDamage>300% damage</style> in an area.
strings.SS2_CYBORG2_PRIMARY_LASTPRISM_DESCRIPTIO: Charge up a continuous laser that deals <style=cIsDamage>1000% damage</style> per second, but has <style=cIsHealth>decreased turning speed.
strings.SS2_CYBORG2_SECONDARY_LASER_DESCRIPTION: Fire laser blasts at up to <style=cIsDamage>3</style> targets, dealing <style=cIsDamage>3x150% damage</style> and <color=#42f5b3>Priming</color> enemies.
strings.SS2_CYBORG2_SECONDARY_MINES_DESCRIPTION: Place a mine that repeatedly <style=cIsDamage>shocks</style> and <color=#42f5b3>Primes</color> nearby targets. Can place up to 3.
strings.SS2_CYBORG2_UTILITY_TELEPORTER_DESCRIPTI: Deploy a <style=cIsUtility>warp point</style>. Reuse to <style=cIsUtility>swap</style> positions with nearby targets.
strings.SS2_CYBORG2_UTILITY_TRAP_DESCRIPTION: Deploy a device that <style=cIsDamage>captures</style> low health enemies, granting <style=cIsUtility>extra money</style> and <style=cIsDamage>elite powers</style> to nearby allies.
strings.SS2_CYBORG2_SPECIAL_MAGNETBALL_DESCRIPTI: Fire an energetic mass for <style=cIsDamage>700% damage</style>. The mass drops shards that explode for <style=cIsDamage>6x175% damage</style> and <color=#42f5b3>Prime</color> enemies.
strings.SS2_ACHIEVEMENT_CYBORG2_MASTERY_DESC: As Cyborg, beat the game or obliterate on Monsoon or harder.
strings.SS2_ACHIEVEMENT_CYBORG2_GRANDMASTERY_DES: As Cyborg, beat the game or obliterate on Typhoon.
strings.SS2_DUT_DESCRIPTION: Seriously this is a very rough early prototype of DU-T, Enjoy it as a novelty, but its buggy.
strings.SS2_EXECUTIONER2_DESCRIPTION:
The Executioner's goal is to spill as much blood as possible in the shortest amount of time. Bullets loaded, ion manipulators charged.

<color=#CCD3E0> < ! > Use Service Pistol to score kills to charge your Ion Manipulators - or charge them manually during downtime.

< ! > Ion Burst is at its strongest when used at maximum charge. Spread its shots across a crowd to maximize its shocking effect.

< ! > Execution is a great crowd control AND single target tool. Don't forget that its damage depends on how many targets it hits!

< ! > Use Crowd Dispersion to manipulate enemies and set them up for a well-placed Execution.
strings.SS2_EXECUTIONER2_OUTRO_FLAVOR: ..and so he left, an empty shell of armor drenched in blood.
strings.SS2_EXECUTIONER2_OUTRO_FAILURE: ..and so he vanished, escaping the inevitable.
strings.SS2_EXECUTIONER2_LORE:
Death is inevitable. It comes for us all. Some may try to evade it, or run from it. But death arrives all the same. Death, however, is simply the cost of war. And death in glorious combat is one of the best deaths a man could ask for.

But, as always, some would seek to run from death. We call them by many names. Deserters, cowards, turncoats. Each and every one of them, traitors to the cause. These traitors must be punished for their crime. And that punishment: in an ironic twist, is the very thing they tried to avoid.

Death will come for them. Not as a robed spectre with a scythe. Not in a trench coat, holding a revolver. Not as a sickness, nor the wear of time. No. For them, death will come bearing lustrous battlegarb, with service pistol loaded, and ion manipulators fully charged.
strings.SS2_KEYWORD_FEAR2: <style=cKeywordName>Fear</style><style=cSub>Causes afflicted enemies to stop attacking and flee. Feared enemies are <style=cIsHealth>instantly killed</style> if below <style=cIsHealth>15%</style> health.</style>
strings.SS2_KEYWORD_EXECUTING2: <style=cKeywordName>Executing</style><style=cSub>Damage and proc coefficient are doubled if no more than one enemy is hit. Kills double gained Ion Manipulator stocks.</style>
strings.SS2_KEYWORD_RECHARGING2: <style=cKeywordName>Recharging</style><style=cSub>This skill can recharge additional stocks by holding the button or killing enemies. Additional stocks increase maximum capacity.</style>
strings.SS2_KEYWORD_IONARMORBUFF: <style=cKeywordName>Ion Armor</style><style=cSub>Increases armor by 60 and decreases skill cooldowns by 20%.</style>
strings.SS2_EXECUTIONER2_EXECUTION_DESC: Leap into the air, then slam an ion axe for <style=cIsDamage>1450% damage</style>. Hitting an isolated target deals <style=cIsDamage>double damage</style> and restores 3 <color=#29e5f2>Ion Charges</color>.
strings.SS2_EXECUTIONER2_EXECUTION_SCEPTER_DESC:
Leap into the air, then slam an ion axe for <style=cIsDamage>1450% damage</style>. Hitting an isolated target deals <style=cIsDamage>double damage</style> and restores 3 <color=#29e5f2>Ion Charges</color>
<color=#d299ff>SCEPTER: Hitting an isolated target also refreshes this skill's cooldown by 35%.</color>
strings.SS2_EXECUTIONER2_FIRINGSQUAD_DESC: <style=cIsDamage>Shocking</style>. Summon a firing squad of ion guns, rapidly firing ion bullets for <style=cIsDamage>350% damage</style> per bullet but losing <style=cIsHealth>-60% movement speed</style>. <style=cIsUtility>Hold to extend duration. Cooldown scales with usage length.</style>
strings.SS2_EXECUTIONER2_IONBURST_DESC: <style=cIsDamage>Shocking</style>. Rapidly discharge <style=cIsUtility>Ion Manipulator stocks</style> as ion bullets for <style=cIsDamage>350% damage</style> per bullet.
strings.SS2_EXECUTIONER2_IONCHARGE_DESC: Rapidly discharge <color=#29e5f2>Ion Charges</color> as ion bullets for <style=cIsDamage>350% damage</style>. <color=#29e5f2>Ion Charges</color> are restored on kill.
strings.SS2_EXECUTIONER2_CONSECRATION_DESC: Consume all <color=#29e5f2>Ion Charges</color> to <style=cIsHealing>heal</style>, gain <style=cIsUtility>movement speed</style>, and reduce your cooldowns by <style=cIsUtility>1 second</style> per charge.
strings.SS2_EXECUTIONER2_DASH_DESC: <style=cIsUtility>Dash</style> forward and inspire <style=cIsUtility>fear</style> in nearby enemies.</style>
strings.SS2_EXECUTIONER2_BLOODLETTING_DESC: Consume <style=cIsHealth>25%</style> of your health to inspire <style=cIsUtility>fear</style> in nearby enemies and</style> gain 3 <color=#29e5f2>Ion Charges</color>.
strings.SS2_EXECUTIONER2_IONJUGGERNAUT_DESC: Swing your ion axe for <style=cIsDamage>1700% damage</style> while held. Activating begins slowly consuming <style=cIsUtility>Ion Manipulator charges</style> to project ion armor.
strings.SS2_EXECUTIONER2_TASER_DESC: Fire arcs of lightning forward for <style=cIsDamage>80% damage</style> that can <style=cIsUtility>chain</style> between enemies up to <style=cIsUtility>4 additional times</style>.
strings.SS2_EXECUTIONER_DESCRIPTION:
The Executioner is a high-risk, high-reward survivor that's all about racking up an endless kill count.

<color=#CCD3E0> < ! > Use Service Pistol to score some kills, with each kill building up an even stronger Ion Burst.

< ! > You can tap Ion Burst to fire a few shots, or hold it to fire them all.

< ! > Execution is a great crowd control AND single target tool. Don't forget that its damage depends on how many targets it hits!

< ! > If you find yourself getting swarmed, Crowd Dispersion can get enemies off your back fast.
strings.SS2_EXECUTIONER_OUTRO_FLAVOR: ..and so he left, an empty shell of armor drenched in blood.
strings.SS2_EXECUTIONER_OUTRO_FAILURE: ..and so he vanished, escaping the inevitable.
strings.SS2_EXECUTIONER_LORE:
Death is inevitable. It comes for us all. Some may try to evade it, or run from it. But death arrives all the same. Death, however, is simply the cost of war. And death in glorious combat is one of the best deaths a man could ask for.

But, as always, some would seek to run from death. We call them by many names. Deserters, cowards, turncoats. Each and every one of them, traitors to the cause. These traitors must be punished for their crime. And that punishment: in an ironic twist, is the very thing they tried to avoid.

Death will come for them. Not as a robed spectre with a scythe. Not in a trench coat, holding a revolver. Not as a sickness, nor the wear of time. No. For them, death will come bearing lustrous battlegarb, with service pistol loaded, and ion manipulators fully charged.
strings.SS2_KEYWORD_FEAR: <style=cKeywordName>Fear</style><style=cSub>Causes afflicted enemies to stop attacking and flee. Enemies afflicted with fear take 50% more damage and have their movement speed slowed by 30%.</style>
strings.SS2_KEYWORD_EXECUTING: <style=cKeywordName>Executing</style><style=cSub>Deals doubled damage if no more than 1 enemy is hit.</style>
strings.SS2_KEYWORD_RECHARGING: <style=cKeywordName>Recharging</style><style=cSub>Killing enemies restores charges of this skill, depending on the strength of the slain enemy. Doubled charges with kills from Execution.</style>
strings.SS2_EXECUTIONER_PASSIVE_DESC: Killing enemies inflicted with <style=cIsUtility>fear</style> reduces all skill cooldowns by <style=cIsUtility>1 second</style>.
strings.SS2_EXECUTIONER_TASER_DESCRIPTION: Fire arcs of lightning forward for <style=cIsDamage>{0}% damage</style>.
strings.SS2_EXECUTIONER_SWINGAXE_DESCRIPTION: Swing your axe for <style=cIsDamage>% damage</style>. <style=cIsUtility>Each Secondary charge grants 4% maximum health in shield</style>.
strings.SS2_EXECUTIONER_IONGUN_DESCRIPTION: <style=cIsUtility>Recharging.</style> Fire a barrage of ionized bullets, dealing <style=cIsDamage>{0}% damage</style> each. <style=cIsUtility>Up to 5 charges are fired at a time</style>.
strings.SS2_EXECUTIONER_IONBLAST_DESCRIPTION: Fire a blast of raw ion energy for <style=cIsDamage>% damage</style>. Can be <style=cIsUtility>charged</style>, using multiple charges at once. <style=cIsUtility>Restocks charges for every enemy you kill, depending on strength</style>.
strings.SS2_EXECUTIONER_DASH_DESCRIPTION: Boost forward and <style=cIsUtility>fear</style> all nearby enemies for <style=cIsUtility>{0} seconds</style>.
strings.SS2_EXECUTIONER_AXE_DESCRIPTION: <style=cIsUtility>Heavy</style>. <style=cIsDamage>Executing.</style> Launch into the air and slam downwards with a massive ion axe for <style=cIsDamage>{0}% damage</style>.
strings.SS2_EXECUTIONER_AXESCEPTER_DESCRIPTION: Launch into the air and slam downward with your ion axe for <style=cIsDamage>3000% damage</style>.
strings.SS2_EXECUTIONER_ARMOR_DESCRIPTION: Equip a suit of ionized armor, giving <style=cIsUtility>60 armor</style> and <style=cIsDamage>supercharging</style> your secondary skill for <style=cIsDamage>4</style> seconds.
strings.SS2_EXECUTIONER_ARMORSCEPTER_NAME: Equip a suit of ionized armor, giving <style=cIsUtility>80 armor</style> and <style=cIsDamage>supercharging</style> your secondary skill for <style=cIsDamage>4</style> seconds. Killing enemies <style=cIsUtility>fears</style> nearby enemies for 4 seconds.
strings.SS2_KNIGHT_DESCRIPTION:
The Knight is a skilled expert in melee combat, dueling and parrying foes with precise timing and execution.

<color=#CCD3E0>< ! > parry.
strings.SS2_KNIGHT_OUTRO_FLAVOR: ..and so he left, battered to the core of hope.
strings.SS2_KNIGHT_OUTRO_FAILURE: ..and so he vanished, shield shattered and hope snuffed out.
strings.SS2_KNIGHT_BODY_LORE: why are there knights in space
strings.SS2_KNIGHT_PASSIVE_DESC: A buff ward surronds you that grants allies in its radius <style=cIsDamage>20% attack speed</style> and <style=cIsDamage>20% damage</style>.
strings.SS2_KNIGHT_PRIMARY_SWORD_DESC: Slice forward for <style=cIsDamage>200% damage</style>. Every third hit stabs forward for <style=cIsDamage>400% damage</style>. While blocking, bash enemies for <style=cIsDamage>400% damage</style>.
strings.SS2_KEYWORD_PARRY: <style=cKeywordName>Parry</style><style=cSub>Block an incoming hit to <style=cIsUtility>stun</style> and deal <style=cIsDamage>350% damage</style> to all enemies around you. You also become <style=cIsUtility>immune to damage</style> for 1 second.</style>
strings.SS2_KNIGHT_SECONDARY_SHIELD_DESC: Raise your shield, gaining <style=cIsUtility>400 armor</style>. Use before an incoming strike to <style=cIsDamage>parry</style> it and <color=#fa78f3>Empower</color> your next skill.
strings.SS2_KNIGHT_SECONDARY_SHIELD_DESC2: Raise your shield, gaining <style=cIsUtility>400 armor</style> and replacing your jump with a <style=cIsUtility>roll</style>. Use before an incoming strike to <style=cIsDamage>parry</style> it and <color=#fa78f3>Empower</color> your next skill.
strings.SS2_KNIGHT_SHIELD_BASH_DESC: Shield Bash in front of you for <style=cIsDamage>240% damage</style> and <style=cIsUtility>stun</style> enemies hit.
strings.SS2_KNIGHT_UTILITY_LEAP_DESC: Dash foward, spinning and slicing repeatedly for <style=cIsDamage>120% damage</style> per hit. The number of hits scales with attack speed.
strings.SS2_KNIGHT_SPECIAL_BANNER_DESC: Thrust a banner into the ground for <style=cIsDamage>750% damage</style>. Nearby allies <style=cIsDamage>smite</style> enemies with their attacks, reducing their armor and <style=cIsHealing>restoring health</style>.
strings.SS2_MULE_DESCRIPTION: Seriously this is a very rough early prototype of an old MULE design for SS2. A full rework is planned, Enjoy it as a novelty, but its buggy.
strings.SS2_MULE_OUTRO_FLAVOR: ..and so it left, engine sputtering, directive finally fulfilled.
strings.SS2_MULE_OUTRO_FAILURE: ..and so it vanished, finally put out of commission.
strings.SS2_MULE_KEYWORD_OVERCLOCKING: <style=cKeywordName>Overclocking</style></style=cSub>Hold the button to extend the skill's duration, but also extend the skill's cooldown length.</sub>
strings.SS2_MULE_KEYWORD_OVERCLOCKING2: <style=cKeywordName>Overclocking</style></style=cSub>Hold the button to charge the skill further, but also extend the skill's cooldown length.</sub>
strings.SS2_MULE_PASSIVE_DRONE_DESC: Taking damage to below <style=cIsHealth>25% health</style>activates your personal <style=cIsHealing>Repair Drone</style>, healing for <style=cIsHealing>50%</style> of your health. Has a <style=cIsUtility>60</style> second cooldown.
strings.SS2_MULE_KEYWORD_PUNCH_CALIBRATED: <style=cKeywordName>Calibrated</style></style=cSub><style=cSub>Slam the ground in front of you, dealing higher damage and sending out a shockwave. (%s tbd)</style>
strings.SS2_MULE_UPHEAVAL_DESC: <style=cIsDamage>Stunning</style>. Punch the ground in front of you, raising a <style=cIsUtility>boulder</style> for <style=cIsDamage>450% damage</style>. <style=cIsDamage>Boulders can be punched to be rolled.</style>
strings.SS2_MULE_KEYWORD_UPHEAVAL_CALIBRATED: <style=cKeywordName>Calibrated</style></style=cSub>Punch the ground in front of you, raising up to 3 boulders at once.
strings.SS2_MULE_OLDPUNCH_DESC: <style=cIsUtility>Overclocking</style>. <style=cIsUtility>Stunning</style>. Wind up, and throw a mighty punch forward for <style=cIsDamage>280% damage</style>. <style=cIsDamage>Damage is multiplied by charge level, up to 4x</style>.
strings.SS2_MULE_JUMP_DESC: <style=cIsUtility>Hold to charge</style> a powerful upwards leap.
strings.SS2_MULE_SPIN_DESC: <style=cIsUtility>Overclocking</style>. Rapidly spin, gradually increasing <style=cIsUtility>movement speed</style> and <style=cIsUtility>increasing armor</style> while dealing <style=cIsDamage>100% damage</style> per rotation.
strings.SS2_MULE_NET_DESC: Launch a <style=cIsUtility>net</style>, holding enemies within <style=cIsUtility>12m</style> of impact in place and preventing them from attacking for <style=cIsUtility>5</style> seconds. <style=cIsUtility>Flying enemies are grounded</style>.
strings.SS2_MULE_CHARGE_DESC: <style=cIsUtility>Calibrating</style>. <style=cIsUtility>Hold to charge up</style>, and release to <style=cIsUtility>fling forward</style> whilst spinning for <style=cIsDamage>(medium-high damage TBD)% damage</style>.
strings.SS2_MULE_KEYWORD_CALIBRATING: <style=cKeywordName>Calibrating</style><style=cSub>Can be canceled with primary, secondary, or jump to perform more powerful "calibrated" variants of those actions.</style>
strings.SS2_NEMBANDIT_DESCRIPTION:
The Nemesis Bandit carefully positions himself via camouflage technology to wrangle foes and cull crowds. 

<color=#CCD3E0>< ! > Show no mercy.
strings.SS2_NEMBANDIT_OUTRO_FLAVOR: ..and so he left, with absolutely no regrets.
strings.SS2_NEMBANDIT_OUTRO_FAILURE: ..and so he returned, luck finally running out.
strings.SS2_NEMBANDIT_PRIMARY_DESCRIPTION: Fire a revolver shot for <style=cIsDamage>220% damage</style>. Can hold up to 6 bullets.
strings.SS2_NEMBANDIT_SPECIAL_DESCRIPTION: Fire a shotgun burst for <style=cIsDamage>6x100% damage</style>. Kills <style=cIsUtility>reset all your cooldowns.</style>
strings.SS2_NEMCAPTAIN_DESCRIPTION:
The Nemesis Captain issues orders to control the battlefield and fight safely from behind his Adaptive Microbots.

<color=#CCD3E0>< ! > Keep your cool. Using Orders too quickly will build Stress, leaving you vulnerable.
strings.SS2_NEMCAPTAIN_OUTRO_FLAVOR: ..and so he left, lighting up a fresh smoke.
strings.SS2_NEMCAPTAIN_OUTRO_FAILURE: ..and so he vanished, retired at last.
strings.SS2_NEMCAPTAIN_LORE: This planet sucks man.. *takes hit from cigar*
strings.SS2_NEMCAPTAIN_STRESSPASSIVE_NAME: idk what to call half of this stuff or how to present it to the player
strings.SS2_NEMCAPTAIN_STRESSPASSIVE_DESC: Gain <style=cIsHealth>Stress</style> while in danger. When Stress reaches maximum, temporarily become <style=cIsHealth>Overstressed</style>, reducing <style=cIsUtility>movement speed</style>, <style=cIsUtility>armor</style>, and <style=cIsDamage>damage</style>.
strings.SS2_NEMCAPTAIN_KEYWORD_STRESS: <style=cKeywordName>Stress</style><style=cSub>Gain Stress by being surrounded by enemies, receiving damage, or using Orders. Reduce Stress by dealing Critical Strikes, healing, killing enemies, using certain Orders, and being out of combat.
strings.SS2_NEMCAPTAIN_KEYWORD_OVERSTRESS: <style=cKeywordName>Overstress</style><style=cSub>Become subjected to the effects of maximum Stress, as well as -30% movement speed, -20 armor, and -50% damage until the Stress meter counts down to 0.
strings.SS2_NEMCAPTAIN_PRIMARY_DESCRIPTION: Rapidly fire for <style=cIsDamage>90% damage</style> per bullet. Can hold up to 20 bullets.
strings.SS2_NEMCAPTAIN_SECONDARY_DESCRIPTION: <style=cIsDamage>Stunning</style>. Arm your cane rifle, then fire a shot for <style=cIsDamage>680% damage</style>.
strings.SS2_NEMCAPTAIN_PUNCH_DESCRIPTION: <style=cIsDamage>Stunning</style>. Throw a punch in front for <style=cIsDamage>150% damage</style>.
strings.SS2_NEMCAPTAIN_HAIDARA_DESCRIPTION: <style=cIsDamage>Shocking</style>. Discharge a thunderbolt in front for <style=cIsDamage>440% damage</style>.
strings.SS2_NEMCAPTAIN_ORDERS_DESCRIPTION: Deploy 1 of 4 <style=cIsUtility>Microbot Orders</style>. Using Orders increases <style=cIsHealth>Stress</style>. <style=cIsUtility>Hold the key</style> of an Order to <style=cIsUtility>discard</style> it.
strings.SS2_NEMCAPTAIN_ORDERNULL_DESCRIPTION: Devise a new plan of attack, slightly increasing <style=cIsHealth>Stress</style>.
strings.SS2_NEMCAPTAIN_ORDERSTRIKE_DESCRIPTION: <style=cIsUtility>Stunning</style>. Aim a Microbot and fire a laser, <style=cIsDamage>exploding</style> for <style=cIsDamage>1400% damage</style>.
strings.SS2_NEMCAPTAIN_ORDERSTRIKETRIPLE_DESCRIP: <style=cIsUtility>Stunning</style>. Aim a Microbot and fire a laser, <style=cIsDamage>exploding</style> for <style=cIsDamage>600% damage</style>. <style=cIsUtility>Can be fired 3 times.</style>
strings.SS2_NEMCAPTAIN_ORDERSTRIKEFROST_DESCRIPT: <style=cIsUtility>Freezing</style>. Aim a Microbot and fire a laser, <style=cIsDamage>exploding</style> for <style=cIsDamage>1100% damage</style>.
strings.SS2_NEMCAPTAIN_ORDERSTRIKESHOCK_DESCRIPT: <style=cIsUtility>Shocking</style>. Aim a Microbot and fire a laser, <style=cIsDamage>exploding</style> for <style=cIsDamage>900% damage</style>.
strings.SS2_NEMCAPTAIN_ORDERREGEN_DESCRIPTION: Temporarily increase the rate at which Stress decreases for <style=cIsUtility>15 seconds</style>.
strings.SS2_NEMCAPTAIN_ORDERREDUCE_DESCRIPTION: Temporarly decrease the amount of Stress gained from all sources by <style=cIsUtility>50%</style> for <style=cIsUtility>10 seconds.</style>
strings.SS2_NEMCAPTAIN_ORDERRECOVER_DESCRIPTION: Massively decrease your current amount of stress, while also <style=cIsUtility>discarding all other Orders</style>. Stress removed is increased per Order lost.
strings.SS2_NEMCAPTAIN_ORDERHEAL_DESCRIPTION: <style=cIsHealing>Heal</style> nearby allies for </style>6%</style> of their <style=cIsHealth>maximum health</style> every second, and also grant <style=cIsUtility>50 armor</style> and <style=cIsDamage>+20% attack speed</style> for <style=cIsUtility>12 seconds</style.
strings.SS2_NEMCOMMANDO_DESCRIPTION:
The Nemesis Commando is a wildcard on the battlefield, capable of turning any situation to his favor.

<color=#CCD3E0>< ! > Single Tap can be used to deal with foes at any distance. Combine it with Blade of Cessation up close to shred foes.

< ! > Gouge is a powerful debuff that can critical hit and activate item effects, on top of its heavy damage.

< ! > Proper balance of melee and ranged combat is key, and Tactical Roll grants high armor to help enter and exit battles.

< ! > Disrupt enemies with Submission, whether it be to aid in an escape or get the jump on an unsuspecting target.
strings.SS2_NEMCOMMANDO_OUTRO_FLAVOR: ..and so he left, newfound sense of humanity within.
strings.SS2_NEMCOMMANDO_OUTRO_FAILURE: ..and so he vanished, humanity lost without a trace.
strings.SS2_NEMCOMMANDO_LORE:
He lays to rest by a fire, setting the weapons he carries within an arm's reach. Checking his surroundings, hoping the cave he hid in would be enough shelter. Some would say it is not wise to be overly cautious. He would say, <link="textShaky">they have not seen enough</link>.

The service pistol he got when he enlisted. Well used, dirtied, and loved. It's brought him through thick and thin, no matter the circumstance. Some would say that only a fool would trust their weapon. He would say, <link="textShaky">your weapon is all you can trust.</link>

The blade of a fallen brother of war. Picked from what he could scavenge of the last of humanity he's seen. Some would say it keeps their spirits united. He would say, <link="textShaky">it keeps their spirits from joining for as long as possible.</link>

The loads of cargo that landed with him. Each item picked from a chest, keeping him alive in one way or another. Dozens of shipments to anyone but him. Some would say they aren't his to take. He would say, <link="textShaky">if he doesn't take them, then no one else will</link>.

The glow of a violet light coming from deeper within the cave. Calming, warming... beckoning. Welcoming him deeper and deeper, promising safety. Some would say that what sounds too good to be true usually is. He would say, <link="textWavy">Faust was a wise man</link>.
strings.SS2_KEYWORD_GOUGE: <style=cKeywordName>Gouge</style><style=cSub>Applies 100% damage over time, capable of critical hits.
strings.SS2_KEYWORD_GOUGE2: <style=cKeywordName>Gouge</style><style=cSub>Applies 100% damage over time. Capable of critical hits and activating item effects.

strings.SS2_NEMMANDO_DESCRIPTION:
The Nemesis Commando is a wildcard on the battlefield, capable of turning any situation to his favor.

<color=#CCD3E0>< ! > Blade of Cessation is risky but can build up a lot of damage by stacking Gouge.

< ! > Distant Gash can be used to safely apply stacks of Gouge to groups of enemies - take advantage of its piercing!

< ! > Proper balance of melee and ranged combat is key, and Tactical Roll grants high armor to help enter and exit battles.

< ! > Submission can be utilized as huge burst of damage, whether it be to aid in an escape or get the jump on an unsuspecting target.
strings.SS2_NEMMANDO_OUTRO_FLAVOR: ..and so he left, newfound sense of humanity within.
strings.SS2_NEMMANDO_OUTRO_FAILURE: ..and so he returned, concluding his endless struggle.
strings.SS2_NEMMANDO_LORE:
He lays to rest by a fire, setting the weapons he carries within an arm's reach. Checking his surroundings, hoping the cave he hid in would be enough shelter. Some would say, it isn't wise to be overly cautious. He would say, <color=#FFCCED>they haven't seen enough.</color>

The service pistol he got when he enlisted. Well used, dirtied, and loved. It's brought him through thick and thin, no matter which mission he took on. Some would say, only a fool would trust their weapon. He would say, <color=#FFCCED>your weapon is all you can trust.</color>

The blade of a fallen brother of war. Picked from what he could scavenge of the last of humanity he's seen. Some would say, it keeps their spirits united. He would say, <color=#FFCCED>it's to keep their spirits from joining for as long as possible.</color>

The loads of cargo that landed with him. Each item picked from a chest, keeping him alive in one way or another. Dozens of shipments to anyone but him. Some would say, they aren't his items to take. He would say, <color=#FFCCED>if he doesn't take them, then no one will.</color>

The glow of a violet light coming from deeper within the cave. Calming, warming... beckoning. Welcoming him deeper and deeper, promising safety. Some would say, what sounds too good to be true probably is. He would say, <color=#FFCCED>Faust was a wise man.</color>

strings.SS2_NEMESIS_MERCENARY_DESCRIPTION:
The Nemesis Mercenary is a fast-paced, short-range survivor, with the tools to dominate any combat encounter - no matter the odds.

strings.SS2_PYRO_DESCRIPTION: The Pyro is a survivor who excels in crowd control, torching pests with fury and precision.
strings.SS2_PYRO_OUTRO_FLAVOR: ..and so he left, his heart burning with passion.
strings.SS2_PYRO_OUTRO_FAILURE: ..and so he vanished, flames finally snuffed out.
strings.SS2_PYRO_LORE:
Buzzing, buzzing, and more buzzing. As they tried to go over the final details of the job, the droning of insect wings was the only sound to break through - but it didn’t matter. The job was simple, and he knew how to see it through properly. The woman in the hardhat proceeded back to the port as the man in polymeric fibers equipped a sizable weapon from his back. Clutching it firmly in one hand, he used the other to manage the cargo bay doors, bracing himself for the swarm within.

A suffocating miasma of acid filled the air as the doors slid open. If not for his respirator, the fumes would’ve been unbearable, toxic enough to knock him out within minutes. The thick air was populated with countless giant green bugs, busily flying between corroded containers and the hive structures they’d built between them. Their incessant droning drowned out his thoughts - just the way he liked it. None of the insects took notice of the intruder until a torrent of napalm exploded into a blaze. 

Warriors descended as workers scattered, though it was too late for all of them. The ones quick enough to form an offense merely hastened their demise, falling to his feet as the fire devoured them. Napalm clung indiscriminately to nest, pest, and security chest alike as he slowly weaved his way between the labyrinth of cargo that laid out the foundations of the lair. Black smoke began to fill the bay, thickening with each step he took, while the ground piled high with charred remains. More than anything, though, it was the heat - the relentless, searing heat of his cleaning flames - that filled the room. Shipping containers turned into furnaces, and the bay a graveyard as he systematically purged the infestation.

Hours later, little was left but the acrid stench of decay, the occasional mandible or thorax, and a fine layer of ash carpeting the floor. After a third lap around the bay, he finally found what he’d been searching for, stashed away behind cracked container doors in the darkest corner of the room: an unharmed nest of eggs. He chuckled to himself, closing the cracked door and submerging the eggs back into darkness. As the large bay doors slammed shut, the woman in the helmet wrinkled her nose, handing him payment for a job well done. And a job well done it was - one hundred percent of the live bugs reduced to an easy clean-up.

As he left the dock, he thought about the bonus he’d claimed for himself: the eggs, healthily growing in their ideal conditions, where regulation couldn’t look until it was too late. There would never be a job as enjoyable as them.

strings.SS2_ACHIEVEMENT_CAPTAIN_GRANDMASTERY_DES: As Captain, beat the game or obliterate on Typhoon or Harder.
strings.SS2_ACHIEVEMENT_HUNTRESS_GRANDMASTERY_DE: As Huntress, beat the game or obliterate on Typhoon or Harder.
strings.SS2_ENGINEER_LASERFOCUS_DESC: Release two beams of energy for <style=cIsDamage>2x150% damage</style>. Damage <style=cIsDamage>increases</style> the <style=cIsUtility>longer a target is focused upon</style>.
strings.SS2_ENGINEER_RAPIDDISPLACEMENT_DESC: <style=cIsDamage>Stunning</style>. <style=cIsUtility>Move forward</style>, pushing any entities in the way for <style=cIsDamage>200% damage</style>.
strings.SS2_BANDIT_TRANQ_GUN_DESC: Fire a tranquilizer dart for <style=cIsDamage>{0}% damage</style>, <style=cIsUtility>slowing down</style> and eventually <style=cIsUtility>stunning</style> enemies. Can hold up 4 darts.
strings.SS2_RAILGUNNER_ROLL_DESC: Roll a short distance and perform a perfect reload if reloading.
